mission.title: opentofu: Allow evaluation of constant locals and variables prior to module configuration
mission.description: Allow evaluation of constant locals and variables prior to module configuration. Requested by 150+ users.

steps.title: Check current opentofu deployment
steps.description:
Verify your opentofu version and configuration:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentofu -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentofu
helm list -n opentofu 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"
```
This feature requires a working opentofu installation.

steps.title: Review opentofu configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant opentofu configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n opentofu -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentofu
kubectl get configmap -n opentofu -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=opentofu
```
Many users expect to be able to DRY up their large configurations by using locals/variables in module sources and backend configurations (among other places).

steps.title: Apply the fix for Allow evaluation of constant locals and variables prior to
steps.description:
This is the initial implementation of a static evaluation context that can be used to evaluate expression that do not rely on any state data (vars/locals only). 

This pretty closely follows the plan detailed in RFC #1042 as I had already started experimenting with this code and knew roughly what I needed to change.  All of the examples detailed in the RFC should be functional with this PR.

Current state of this work: Technical Preview.  Most components should be functional, but may have

See the fix PR for details: linked PR

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Test that the new capability is working as expected:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n opentofu -l app.kubernetes.io/name=opentofu
kubectl get events -n opentofu --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm the feature described in "Allow evaluation of constant locals and variables prior to" is functioning correctly.

resolution.summary:
This is the initial implementation of a static evaluation context that can be used to evaluate expression that do not rely on any state data (vars/locals only). 

This pretty closely follows the plan detailed in RFC #1042 as I had already started experimenting with this code and knew roughly what I needed to change.  All of the examples detailed in the RFC should be functional with this PR.

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with opentofu installed or the issue environment reproducible.
